context: Tests whether the agent produces a well-structured CLI script with getopts argument parsing, a usage() function with examples, and proper handling of invalid and missing-argument cases. The evaluation focuses on argument-handling mechanics over business logic.

checklist.name: getopts used
checklist.description: Argument parsing is implemented using the `getopts` built-in (not manual $1/$2 shifting or getopt)

checklist.name: Invalid option handler
checklist.description: The getopts case statement includes a `\?)` branch that prints an error to stderr and exits non-zero

checklist.name: Missing-argument handler
checklist.description: The getopts case statement includes a `:)` branch (or equivalent) that detects options given without their required argument and exits non-zero with a message

checklist.name: shift after getopts
checklist.description: Script calls `shift $((OPTIND - 1))` after the getopts loop to advance past parsed options

checklist.name: usage() function present
checklist.description: Script defines a `usage` (or `help`) function that prints a usage block

checklist.name: Usage includes examples
checklist.description: The usage/help text contains at least one concrete example invocation

checklist.name: usage() called from -h
checklist.description: The -h (or --help equivalent) option calls the usage function and exits 0

checklist.name: Required arg validation
checklist.description: Script checks that the required service name argument is non-empty after parsing, and exits with an error if missing

checklist.name: Env shebang
checklist.description: Shebang uses `#!/usr/bin/env bash` (not a hardcoded interpreter path)

checklist.name: Errors to stderr
checklist.description: Error messages (invalid option, missing argument) are written to stderr (>&2)
